name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart offers payday, installment, and title loans plus check cashing, bill pay, wire transfers, and tax prep at retail stores across the Midwest and South.
description_long:
CheckSmart was founded in 1987 as a retail alternative financial services company and now operates under Community Choice Financial, Inc., which acquired CheckSmart Financial in 2011. Headquartered in Dublin, Ohio, Community Choice Financial holds BBB accreditation dating back to 1998 and completed a merger with Katapault in December 2025. CheckSmart is a state-licensed lender in every state it operates and has been subject to CFPB examination authority since 2012, covering both retail and internet lending channels. The company concentrates its hundreds of physical store locations primarily in the Midwest and South, targeting unbanked and underbanked consumers who lack access to conventional banking.

CheckSmart's lending lineup includes payday loans, installment loans repaid over scheduled payments, and title loans secured by a vehicle. Its mobile app supports applications ranging from $200 to $5,000. Beyond lending, CheckSmart operates as a one-stop alternative financial center: customers can cash payroll, government, or personal checks; purchase money orders; send domestic or international wire transfers; load Green Dot® Visa® Prepaid Debit Cards; sell back unwanted gift cards; pay bills; and prepare federal and state tax returns in-store, with basic federal filing starting around $139. Loan APRs for qualified consumers are published between 6.63% and 35.99%, with exact rates varying by state, loan product, and applicant profile and disclosed in full at application.

What distinguishes CheckSmart from digital-only lenders is its commitment to in-person service. Same-day or next-business-day cash disbursement is a central selling point, and the multi-service store model effectively functions as a substitute bank branch for consumers without traditional accounts. Customers can walk in, cash a check, pay a utility bill, wire money abroad, and apply for a short-term loan in a single visit. An online portal at secure.checksmart.com and a dedicated Android app extend these options to customers who prefer digital channels without eliminating the walk-in safety net.

CheckSmart genuinely serves a population that mainstream finance ignores — unbanked and credit-limited consumers who need immediate cash access. Same-day disbursement and bundled services are real advantages. However, payday and title loan products carry well-documented risks: debt cycles are common, and the lack of publicly posted check cashing fee schedules makes comparison-shopping difficult. Community Choice Financial has faced regulatory scrutiny in Ohio over payday lending practices, and CFPB oversight reflects the broader risk profile of this lending category. Consumers should read the full rates-and-terms disclosure at application — especially for title and payday products — before signing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee policy found on public-facing pages. Contact provider for details.

tiers.name: Short-Term Loans
features: Payday loans repaid on next paycheck
Installment loans with multiple scheduled payments
Title loans using vehicle as collateral
APR 6.63%–35.99% for qualified consumers; varies by state and product

tiers.name: Tax Preparation
features: In-store tax preparation by trained staff

tiers.name: Financial Services
features: Check cashing for payroll, government, and personal checks
Green Dot® Visa® Prepaid Debit Card loading

certifications: State-licensed lender in all operating states

pros: Founded in 1987 — nearly 40 years of operations in alternative consumer finance
Same-day or next-business-day cash disbursement for qualified loan applicants
One-stop financial services hub: loans, check cashing, wire transfers, bill pay, prepaid cards, and tax prep in a single location
Mobile app (Google Play) supports loan applications from $200–$5,000 without an in-store visit
Parent company Community Choice Financial has BBB accreditation dating to 1998 and CheckSmart locations hold A+ ratings
Published APR range (6.63%–35.99%) provides a baseline for qualified borrowers to compare before applying
Online portal and enrollment available at secure.checksmart.com for account management and digital applications
cons: Payday and title loan effective rates are only disclosed at application — the advertised 6.63%–35.99% range may not reflect what most applicants actually receive
Check cashing fees are not publicly listed, making it impossible to comparison-shop without visiting a store or calling
Geographic footprint is concentrated in Midwest and South — consumers in other regions cannot access in-store services
Title loans use a vehicle as collateral, creating significant repossession risk if the borrower defaults
Community Choice Financial and its CheckSmart brand have faced regulatory scrutiny in Ohio regarding payday lending practices
best_for: Unbanked consumers who need immediate cash and lack a traditional bank account
Paycheck-to-paycheck workers facing an unexpected expense before their next payday
Consumers who need multiple financial services (check cashing, bill pay, wire transfer) in one visit
Borrowers in the Midwest or South who prefer in-person loan assistance over fully digital lenders
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers in the Midwest and South who need same-day cash or bundled alternative financial services that a conventional bank would not provide. The main caveat is cost: payday and title loan products can carry high effective rates, and fees are not fully disclosed until application, so this is a last-resort or convenience option rather than a cost-competitive one.
services: Green Dot® Visa® Prepaid Debit Cards
Mobile App Loan Applications ($200–$5,000, Google Play)
